pattern: A single left-to-right pass merges equal adjacent pairs into doubled values and zeros, after which a stable compaction slides every zero to the tail.
hints: The operations are applied in order on the original positions, so a left-to-right scan over adjacent pairs is enough.
When two neighbours are equal, the left one doubles and the right one becomes zero; this can chain into the next pair.
After the merge pass, compact the array keeping non-zero values in their relative order and append the zeros at the end.

edge_inputs.description: chained merges from the worked example
minimum length, only a shift needed
all equal values merge greedily but not all at once
no equal neighbours, only zero shift
leading zeros that all sink to the end
merge produces an interior zero to push back
two max values merge to double

stress.description: random array over a tiny value range to force frequent merges
